_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 7-11 (Northern Virginia / Fairfax) — Karen Keys-Gamarra (HD-7), Irene Shin (HD-8), Karrie Delaney (HD-9), Dan Helmer (HD-10), Gretchen Bulova (HD-11). All five Democrats. Evidence-first finalization (state tier) from primary roll-call records (LIS / VPAP) plus issue-org records (ReproRising/ChoiceTracker, Giffords/Everytown). Each FALSE cites a documented vote/sponsorship. Routine near-unanimous bipartisan votes (e.g. SB778 obscenity-to-minors 96-0, HB131 religious accommodation 94-2, SJ59 Day of Prayer 99-0) were left NULL per the high-bar credit rule, not counted as alignment. HJ2 felon-voting amendment does not map cleanly to the five election_integrity questions, so left null where it was the only election evidence. Helmer's individual same-sex-marriage roll call could not be confirmed (he is not a patron of the marriage amendment and LIS vote pages are JS-rendered), so his biblical_marriage cells are null, not inferred.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-7 (Fairfax County); attorney (family law, court-appointed counsel for children); twice-elected At-Large Fairfax County School Board member (2017-2024); former Planning Commissioner. Assumed HD-7 Jan 2024; won reelection Nov 4, 2025. B.A. Tulane; J.D. Washington University in St. Louis.

karen-keys-gamarra.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-7 (Fairfax County); attorney, former Fairfax County School Board member; reelected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive record on the flagship 2026-ballot amendments: co-patron/supporter of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, House 51-48 in 2025; advanced again 64-34 in 2026) and the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, House 67-31). Co-sponsor of the felon voting-rights restoration amendment (HJ2). Strongly pro-gun-control: CHIEF PATRON of HB207 (2026), a $500 retail tax on firearm suppressors, and a patron of Del. Helmer's HB217 assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban; endorsed by Everytown/Moms Demand Action. CROSS-CURRENT NOTE (documented, but maps to no rubric cell): chief patron of HB994 (2024), the bill banning child marriage by setting the marriage age at 18 with no exceptions — bipartisan, signed by Republican Gov. Youngkin; a genuine child-protection law. No conservative deviations on the scored dimensions. Cells with no public record (Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration roll calls, election-admin specifics) left null, not inferred.

0.note: Co-patron/supporter of HJ1, the constitutional amendment establishing a fundamental right to reproductive freedom (abortion); does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Backs the reproductive-freedom amendment — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ3, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's one-man-one-woman (Marshall-Newman) marriage clause.

1.note: Supports codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Chief patron of HB207 (suppressor tax); patron of the HB217 assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban; Everytown/Moms Demand-endorsed — opposes deregulated firearm access.

profile.religion: Reported Buddhist (Wikipedia; not confirmed by a primary/self-authored source)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-8 (Fairfax County — Herndon/Reston/Chantilly); first elected 2021 (old HD-86), to HD-8 since Jan 2024; reelected 2025. First Korean American woman in the VA House. Former Executive Director of the Virginia Civic Engagement Table (voter-engagement nonprofit). Ran for the VA-11 U.S. House special election in 2025 and lost the Democratic primary; remains the sitting HD-8 delegate.

irene-shin.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-8 (Fairfax County); former nonprofit director; reelected Nov 2025; lost the 2025 VA-11 congressional primary, remains the sitting delegate. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive record: voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, party-line 51-48), the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9, 58-35), and the felon voting-rights amendment (HJ2, 55-44). Rated a 'Pro-Choice Champion' by Virginia Choice Tracker; member of the Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia and NOW-VA. Co-patron of the 2024 assault-weapons ban (HB2); voted for a firearm-violence-prevention office (HB1736); Giffords PAC 'gun safety champion.' Cosponsored the $15 minimum-wage bill (HB1, 2024, vetoed). No conservative deviations found. Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, and immigration roll calls had no citable Shin-specific vote and are left null, not inferred.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment, party-line 51-48); 'Pro-Choice Champion' per Virginia Choice Tracker — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the full pro-abortion-access slate (contraception mandates YES; abortion restrictions NO) — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ9, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban (House 58-35).

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Co-patron of the 2024 assault-weapons ban (HB2); voted for a firearm-violence-prevention office; Giffords PAC 'gun safety champion' — supports assault-weapons ban, red-flag/ERPO, and universal background checks.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-9 (western Fairfax County — Centreville/Chantilly); in office since 2017; won 2025 reelection (term to Jan 2028). Chair of House Transportation; also Courts of Justice and General Laws. Former communications director for Shared Hope International (anti-trafficking nonprofit); former West Melbourne, FL city councilmember (as a Republican) before moving to Virginia and being elected as a Democrat. VPAP lists 98% Democratic-caucus voting unity.

karrie-delaney.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-9 (western Fairfax); in office since 2017; reelected Nov 2025; 98% Democratic-caucus unity (VPAP). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deeply documented progressive record from her individual LIS roll calls: YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, 2025 & 2026), the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026), the $15 minimum-wage bill (HB1), and a full slate of ballot-access expansions (Sunday early voting SB438, extended absentee-receipt deadline HB82, ballot-cure HB773/774, automatic-voter-registration work group HB319, ranked-choice HB630). Voted YES on HB1441 limiting local law-enforcement (287g) cooperation with federal immigration authorities. Gun-control: YES on the HB217 assault-weapons/large-magazine ban, red-flag reporting (HB1096), safe-storage (HB871/HB201), and ghost-gun ban (HB40); Giffords PAC-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT NOTE (routine near-unanimous votes, left NULL per the high-bar credit rule, not counted as alignment): YES on SB778 (display of obscene material to a minor unlawful, 96-0), HB131 (religious-observance accommodation at public colleges, 94-2), and several minor pro-law-enforcement administrative bills. No genuine conservative deviation on any scored dimension.

0.note: Recorded YES on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom (abortion) constitutional amendment, both 2025 (51-48) and 2026 (64-34) passages.

1.note: Voted the pro-abortion-access slate (contraception statutes/mandates YES); featured by REPRO Rising Virginia — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Recorded YES on HJ9 (2025, 58-35) and HJ3 (2026, 67-31), the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban.

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted YES on SB438 (early in-person Sunday voting) and HB82 (extended absentee-ballot receipt deadline) — expands voting beyond single-day in-person.

3.note: Voted to expand mail/absentee voting — YES on the ballot-cure process (HB773/774) and the extended absentee-receipt deadline (HB82); opposite of opposing mass mail-in voting. [source: LIS member roll-call record]

2.note: Voted YES on HB1441, restricting local law-enforcement 287(g)-style cooperation with federal immigration authorities — a pro-sanctuary posture, opposite of opposing sanctuary policy.

1.note: Voted YES on the HB217 assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban, red-flag reporting (HB1096), safe-storage (HB871/HB201), and the ghost-gun ban (HB40); Giffords PAC-endorsed.

profile.religion: Jewish (grandson of Holocaust survivors; son of an Israeli father)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-10 (Fairfax County / Fairfax Station); in office since 2020 (old HD-40, to HD-10 since Jan 2024). West Point graduate, Rhodes Scholar, retired U.S. Army Reserve Lieutenant Colonel (Iraq/Afghanistan/Korea). House Democratic Caucus Campaigns Chair. Now running for U.S. House in the newly drawn VA-7 (2026 Democratic primary).

dan-helmer.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-10 (Fairfax County); Army veteran (West Point, LTC, ret.); House Dem Caucus Campaigns Chair; running for Congress (VA-7, 2026). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] A leading progressive: CHIEF PATRON of HB217 (2026), the assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban now signed into law (Chapter 1106) — his quote: 'weapons similar to those I carried in Iraq and Afghanistan should not be trafficked in our commonwealth.' Multiply Giffords/Brady-endorsed. Co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) and the felon voting-rights amendment (HJ2), both headed to the Nov 2026 ballot; Planned Parenthood-endorsed. CORRECTION captured during research: an iVoterGuide-derived snippet suggesting he 'supports school choice' is a misread — he did NOT answer that questionnaire; not scored. DISCIPLINE NOTE: his individual same-sex-marriage roll call could not be confirmed (he is not a patron of the marriage amendment and LIS vote pages are JS-rendered), so biblical_marriage is left null rather than inferred from party. Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, family/child, and immigration cells had no confirmed Helmer-specific vote and are null.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom (abortion) constitutional amendment; Planned Parenthood-endorsed — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Campaigns on securing abortion rights in the state constitution — opposite of abortion abolition.
CHIEF PATRON of HB217, the assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban signed into law (Chapter 1106, 2026); Giffords/Brady-endorsed — the leading edge of the gun-restriction position.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-11 (City of Fairfax + part of Fairfax County). Sworn in Jan 14, 2026 after winning the Jan 13, 2026 special election (68.8%) to succeed her husband David Bulova (appointed Secretary of Natural & Historic Resources). Career museum/history administrator — Director of the Office of Historic Alexandria since 2019; no prior elected office. B.A. William & Mary; M.A. George Washington University.

gretchen-bulova.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-11 (City of Fairfax + part of Fairfax County); sworn in Jan 14, 2026 via special election to succeed her husband David Bulova; career museum administrator, no prior elected office. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Short but real 2026-session voting record, confirmed from individual LIS roll calls: YEA on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment, 64-34), YEA on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment, 67-31), YEA on HJ2 (felon voting-rights amendment, 65-33) — all on 01/14/2026 — and YEA on HB217 (assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban, 58-34) on 02/05/2026. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, REPRO Rising VA, and Moms Demand Action. On all four contested culture-war roll calls she voted with the Democratic majority; no cross-party deviation. (ROSTER FIX: she represents the City of Fairfax, not Alexandria, and was never 'Mayor of Alexandria' — she held no prior elective office.) Dimensions with no public record (Christian liberty, family/child, federal overreach, immigration, election-admin specifics) left null, not inferred.

0.note: Recorded YEA on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom (abortion) constitutional amendment (House 64-34, 01/14/2026) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia and REPRO Rising; campaigns to 'protect choice' via the amendment — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Recorded YEA on HJ3, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban (House 67-31, 01/14/2026).

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Recorded YEA on HB217, the assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban (House 58-34, 02/05/2026); endorsed by Moms Demand Action — supports the gun-restriction position.
